procedure_name: Bankruptcy ODP Charge Off QC Procedure
description: Validate that ODP bankruptcy accounts are properly charged off, charged off within SLA, and tagged correctly for bankruptcy.

agents.purpose: Retrieves structured population batches of bankruptcy ODP accounts. Performs no QC judgment.
Gathers raw evidence for one account using registered evidence tools. Returns evidence only.
Evaluates evidence and rules to produce step-level or final-level QC decisions. Does not retrieve data.

population_phase.description: Retrieve the bankruptcy ODP accounts to be reviewed. The orchestrator runs this phase once per batch before entering the account phase.

steps.title: Load bankruptcy ODP population batch
steps.objective: Retrieve a paginated batch of bankruptcy ODP accounts in scope for this QC run.

account_phase.description: For each account in the population batch, the orchestrator runs evidence collection, then step-level decisions, then the final account decision. Steps are driven by depends_on and step_type — the orchestrator must not hardcode a fixed sequence.

steps.title: Collect charge-off status evidence
steps.objective: Gather evidence showing whether the account has an explicit charge-off status code in the source system.
steps.checkpoint_note: In checkpoint scope, run for the first account only.

steps.title: Evaluate account charged-off check
steps.objective: Apply rule_chargeoff_status to the charge-off status evidence collected in acct-1a. Return a step-level decision.
steps.orchestration_hint: Call make_qc_decision with decision_mode=step_decision. Pass account_context, evidence_bundle from acct-1a, and the rule subset for evaluation_rule_ids (rule_chargeoff_status only).

steps.title: Collect bankruptcy and charge-off timing evidence
steps.objective: Gather the bankruptcy notification date and charge-off date using get_bankruptcy_notification_and_chargeoff_dates, then call calculate_days_between_dates to compute the number of calendar days between them. Both tools must be called in sequence — dates first, then the day-difference calculation.
steps.orchestration_hint: Step 1: call get_bankruptcy_notification_and_chargeoff_dates(account_number) to retrieve bankruptcy_notification_date and charge_off_date. Step 2: call calculate_days_between_dates(start_date=bankruptcy_notification_date, end_date=charge_off_date) to compute the days_difference. The evidence bundle must include the output of both tool calls.

steps.title: Evaluate charge-off timeliness check
steps.objective: Apply rule_chargeoff_within_sla to the timing evidence from acct-2a. Return a step-level decision.
steps.orchestration_hint: Call make_qc_decision with decision_mode=step_decision. Pass account_context, evidence_bundle from acct-2a (which contains outputs of both get_bankruptcy_notification_and_chargeoff_dates and calculate_days_between_dates), and the rule subset (rule_chargeoff_within_sla only). The evidence_bundle will contain a check='date_difference_calculation' entry with a days_difference field — compare against sla_days_threshold=60.

steps.title: Collect bankruptcy tag evidence
steps.objective: Gather evidence showing whether the expected bankruptcy tag is present for the account.

steps.title: Evaluate bankruptcy tag check
steps.objective: Apply rule_bankruptcy_tag to the tag evidence from acct-3a. Return a step-level decision.
steps.orchestration_hint: Call make_qc_decision with decision_mode=step_decision. Pass account_context, evidence_bundle from acct-3a, and the rule subset (rule_bankruptcy_tag only). Check expected_tag_present against expected_tag_value 'Confirmed BK via Scrub'.

steps.title: Determine final QC verdict for account
steps.objective: Aggregate step decisions from acct-1b, acct-2b, and acct-3b into one final QC verdict for the account.
steps.orchestration_hint: Call make_qc_decision with decision_mode=final_decision. Pass account_context, step_decisions=[acct-1b result, acct-2b result, acct-3b result], and evaluation_rules for rule_final_aggregation.

evaluation_rules.title: Account charged-off check
evaluation_rules.description: The account must have an explicit CHARGE OFF status code in the source used by the business process.

decision_matrix.note: Account has CHARGE OFF status code — check passes
Account does not have CHARGE OFF status code — QC failure

evaluation_rules.title: Charge-off within SLA
evaluation_rules.description: The account must be charged off within 60 days of the bankruptcy notification date. The qc_validation_agent must first call get_bankruptcy_notification_and_chargeoff_dates to retrieve raw dates, then call calculate_days_between_dates to compute days_difference. The decision agent evaluates days_difference against the 60-day threshold.

decision_matrix.evidence_condition: days_difference is null or calculation_successful is false
decision_matrix.note: A required date is missing or the calculation failed — SLA cannot be assessed; treat as QC failure
Charged off within 60 days of bankruptcy notification — SLA met
Charged off more than 60 days after bankruptcy notification — SLA exceeded

evaluation_rules.title: Bankruptcy tag check
evaluation_rules.description: The account must be tagged with exactly 'Confirmed BK via Scrub'. Other BK-related tags are not sufficient.

decision_matrix.note: Expected tag 'Confirmed BK via Scrub' is present — check passes
Expected tag 'Confirmed BK via Scrub' is not present — QC failure

evaluation_rules.title: Final verdict aggregation
evaluation_rules.description: Aggregates the three step-level decisions into one final account verdict. Apply the aggregation policy defined in the prompt.

decision_policy.description: The orchestrator may call the decision agent dynamically whenever sufficient evidence exists for a step. Step decisions are not batched — they are triggered per account as soon as the required depends_on steps are complete. The final decision is triggered after all three step decisions are complete.

result_schema.description: Defines the expected fields of each per-account QC result produced by the orchestrator. The orchestrator must populate all account_level_fields for every successfully processed account.

account_level_fields.description: The account identifier from the population phase.
Full account context from the population output. Must include: account_number, borrower_name, co_borrower_name, bankruptcy_chapter, balance, reason, as_of_date.
Keyed by step_id. Contains the raw evidence bundle returned by each evidence_collection step.
Keyed by step_id. Contains the decision string (pass / fail / manual_review / error) for each step_decision step. Does NOT include the final_decision step.
Keyed by step_id. Contains one concise reason string for each step_decision step.
Step decision from acct-1b — whether the account is marked as charged off. Values: pass, fail, manual_review, error.
Step decision from acct-2b — whether the account was charged off within the 60-day SLA. Values: pass, fail, manual_review, error.
Step decision from acct-3b — whether the account is tagged with 'Confirmed BK via Scrub'. Values: pass, fail, manual_review, error.
Aggregated final verdict from acct-4. Allowed values: pass, fail, manual_review.
Concise plain-text explanation of the final verdict.

execution_status.note: manual_review must only appear inside final_decision or step_decisions. It must never be used as execution status. System status and business decision are always separate.

error_schema.description: Populated only when status is error. Never populated on a completed run.

fields.description: Short error class identifier (e.g. tool_failure, schema_error, timeout).
Human-readable explanation of what went wrong.
The step_id at which the failure occurred, if known. Omit if not applicable.

checkpoint_scope.description: Limits for local testing and flow validation. Not for production use.
checkpoint_scope.selection_rule: process the first account in the first batch only
